title: Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected wildlife sanctuary in the southern state of Karnataka in India.
overview.description: Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected wildlife sanctuary in the southern state of Karnataka in India. It derives its name from the presiding deity "Goddess Mookambika" of the popular Kollur Mookambika Temple. The sanctuary lies in the Western Ghats in Udupi district of Karnataka. The sanctuary consists of an area of 274 km2 (106 sq mi) was notified in the year 1974, by the Government of Karnataka vide notification AFD.48.FWL.74 dated 17 June 1974.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Maravanthe
topPlaces.description: Maravanthe is a village and a beach in Byndoor Taluk, Udupi District, Karnataka, India. NH-66 (formerly NH-17) runs next to the beach and the Souparnika River flows on the other side of the road.

topPlaces.name: Trasi
topPlaces.description: Trasi is a village on the west coast of India, 12 km north of Kundapur in Kundapura Taluk, Udupi District.

topPlaces.name: Padukone
topPlaces.description: Padukone is a coastal village in Byndoor Taluk of Udupi district in the Indian state of Karnataka. The village lies near Maravanthe and is situated along the banks of the Souparnika River, which surrounds parts of the settlement and contributes to its distinctive riverine landscape.

topPlaces.name: Badakere
topPlaces.description: Badakere is a village in the Kundapur taluk of Udupi district, Karnataka, India. As of 2011, Badakere has a total population of 1,711 people consisting of 974 male and 156 female residents.

topPlaces.name: Gangolli
topPlaces.description: Gangolli (also Ganguli) is a town in Kundapur Taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka state. It is situated at the estuary of the Panchagangavalli River.

topPlaces.name: Uppinakudru
topPlaces.description: Uppinakudru, is a small island located six kilometres north of Kundapura town in Udupi District of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Panchagangavalli River
topPlaces.description: Panchagangavalli River is a river flowing through Kundapur and Gangolli in western India. The five rivers namely Souparnika River, Varahi River, Kedaka River, Chakra River and Kubja River join and merge into Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Kemmannu
topPlaces.description: Kemmannu-Hoode-Bengre is a small village in the Udupi District of Karnataka State. It lies on the western coast of Karnataka, along the Suvarna River.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (86 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (95 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Maravanthe, Trasi, Padukone, Badakere, Gangolli.
